terms.term: DRY / Small Functions / Law of Demeter
terms.what: DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself): extract duplicated logic into shared methods/classes. Small functions: do one thing, fit one screen. Law of Demeter: talk to direct friends only — avoid obj.getA().getB().doThing() (train wreck).
terms.why: DRY: change one place, all usages benefit. Small functions: easy to test, name, understand. Law of Demeter: reduces coupling — each object depends only on direct neighbors.
terms.how: DRY: extract method. Small: if > 20 lines, split. LoD: order.getCustomer().getName() — Order knows Customer — OK. order.getCustomer().getAddress().getCity().toUpperCase() — Order knows Address details — violation.
terms.where: All code. Method extraction for DRY. Service design for LoD — expose what you need via direct methods.
terms.when: DRY: whenever you copy-paste code. Small: when function description needs 'AND'. LoD: when chaining more than 2 dots.
terms.issue: LoD violation: chained calls tightly couple to internal structure. Change in City breaks Order-level code.
terms.fix: Order.getCustomerCity() — Order facade hides navigation. Only one method needs to change if City moves.

star.a: Extracted order.getCustomerShippingCity() method. Internal chain hidden.

terms.what: CQS: a method either changes state (command) OR returns data (query) — never both. Commands are void. Queries have no side effects.
terms.why: Methods that both change state and return values are hard to reason about. Side effects in getters cause bugs.
terms.how: Bad: int getAndIncrementCounter() — returns AND changes. Good: int getCounter() (query) + void incrementCounter() (command). Spring Data: @Query for reads, @Modifying for writes.
terms.where: Service methods, getter methods, repository queries.
terms.when: Default: separate read and write. Exception: Optional.orElseGet (acceptable convention break). Builder.build() returns and doesn't mutate (OK — final step).
terms.issue: User.setAndGetId() — sets ID and returns it — side effect in a getter-like method causes confusion.
terms.fix: Separate: setId(id) + getId(). Or use Builder pattern for construction.

star.s: Code review: loadAndTransformData() method changes DB state AND returns result.
star.a: Split into loadData() (query) + transformData(data) (pure function) + saveResult(data) (command).

terms.what: Magic numbers: unnamed constants (86400, 0.15, 100) — replace with named constants. Positive conditionals: 'if(isValid())' is clearer than 'if(!isInvalid())'.
terms.why: Magic numbers: reader has no idea what 86400 means. Named constant makes intent clear. Positive conditionals: double negation requires mental translation.
terms.how: Bad: if(count > 100) → Good: if(count > MAX_PAGE_SIZE). Bad: if(!user.isNotActive()) → Good: if(user.isActive()). final static int MAX_PAGE_SIZE = 100;
terms.where: All code. Constants extracted to top of class or constants file.
terms.when: Every numeric literal (except 0 and 1 in simple increments) should be a named constant.
terms.issue: Magic number changed in one place but same literal appears elsewhere — inconsistent behavior.
terms.fix: Named constant changed in one place affects all usages.

star.s: Code review: 86400 appearing 6 times in codebase.
star.r: One constant, all usages clear and consistent.

terms.what: Multi-stage build: use one Docker stage to build (JDK, Maven), a separate final stage to run (JRE only). Build artifacts copied from build stage to run stage. Final image has no build tools.
terms.why: Build image with Maven+JDK is ~600MB. Final runtime image with JRE is ~100MB. Smaller image = faster pulls, smaller attack surface.
terms.how: FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21 AS builder COPY . . RUN mvn package. FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre COPY --from=builder /app/target/app.jar /app.jar CMD ['java','-jar','/app.jar']
terms.where: All production Docker builds for Java applications.
terms.when: Always use multi-stage for Java — build stage needs Maven/JDK, run stage only needs JRE.
terms.issue: Single-stage build: final image includes Maven, JDK, source code — 600MB+ and security risk.
terms.fix: Split into builder stage (Maven+JDK) and runtime stage (JRE only). Copy only the final JAR.

star.a: Multi-stage build. Final image is JRE + JAR only.

terms.what: Blue-Green: two identical environments (Blue=current, Green=new). Switch traffic instantly to Green. Easy rollback: switch back to Blue. Canary: gradually shift traffic percentage to new version. Rolling: replace instances one by one.
terms.why: Zero-downtime deployments. Blue-Green: instant rollback. Canary: detect issues early with small % of traffic. Rolling: gradual with no extra environment needed.
terms.how: Blue-Green: load balancer switches Blue→Green. Canary: LB sends 5% to new, 95% to old. Increase gradually. Rolling: K8s default strategy.
terms.where: Production deployments. Kubernetes supports all three natively.
terms.when: Blue-Green for zero-downtime with instant rollback (needs 2x infrastructure). Canary for risk management. Rolling for resource-efficient deployments.
terms.issue: Blue-Green: DB schema must be backward compatible — both Blue and Green run simultaneously during switch.
terms.fix: Expand-contract migration: add new columns first (both work), switch traffic, remove old columns later.

star.s: Deployment causes 5-minute downtime — users see errors.
star.a: Blue-Green deployment. Deploy to Green. Switch load balancer. Rollback in 30 seconds if issue.

terms.what: SonarQube: static code analysis — detects bugs, code smells, security vulnerabilities, duplicate code. JaCoCo: Java code coverage tool — measures % of code exercised by tests.
terms.why: Automated code quality gate — catches issues before production. Coverage enforces testing discipline.
terms.how: Maven: add JaCoCo plugin, sonar-maven-plugin. Run: mvn test jacoco:report sonar:sonar. SonarQube shows: coverage %, critical bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells.
terms.where: CI/CD pipeline — run on every commit. Block PRs with quality gate failures.
terms.when: Integrate in CI from day one. Set quality gate: coverage > 80%, 0 critical bugs.
terms.issue: High line coverage but low branch coverage — if-else branches not tested. Real bugs hide in untested branches.
terms.fix: Configure JaCoCo for branch coverage. Set branch coverage gate in SonarQube: > 70%.

star.s: Bug in production despite 90% line coverage.
star.a: SonarQube shows branch coverage only 45%. Null-handling branch never tested. Added test.
